evidence: The paper uses permutation importance (not impurity importance): "we used permutation importance to identify which variables were most influential" and Figure 1 displays "Scaled Permutation Importance Scores." Although the paper ranks and interprets importance magnitudes extensively, conclusions are well-corroborated by accumulated local effects, 10-fold cross-validation on held-out test folds, bivariate group comparisons, and local ceteris-paribus profiles examining how changes in variables affect individual predictions. Impurity-importance bias cannot affect permutation-importance-based conclusions.
